title: Example API — Checkout & Billing Flow
subtitle: A single business-logic path traced end to end: cart → validate → charge → persist → notify.
summary: Flow mode scopes the map to the stages of ONE pipeline, left to right, rather than the whole system. Use it to answer "what happens when a customer checks out?"

nodes.label: Checkout request
nodes.description: Authenticated request with a cart id and a payment method.
decisions: Idempotency key required so retries don't double-charge.

nodes.label: Validate cart
nodes.description: Re-checks item availability and recomputes the price server-side; never trusts the client total.
decisions: Pricing is always recomputed server-side to prevent tampering.

nodes.label: Create intent
nodes.description: Creates a payment intent for the validated total.
decisions: Amount comes from the server-side price, in minor units.

nodes.label: Stripe
nodes.description: Processes the charge and later posts a webhook with the result.
decisions: Source of truth for payment status is the webhook, not the sync response.

nodes.label: Save order
nodes.description: Writes the order row in a pending state; the webhook flips it to paid.
decisions: Order is written before payment confirms so a dropped webhook can be reconciled.

nodes.label: Payment webhook
nodes.description: Verifies the Stripe signature, then advances the order to paid (idempotently).
decisions: Idempotent so duplicate/out-of-order deliveries are safe.

nodes.label: Send receipt
nodes.description: On paid, emails the customer a receipt and emits an analytics event.
decisions: Sent from the webhook path so it only fires on confirmed payment.

flows.label: Checkout
flows.title: A checkout, end to end
steps: Customer POSTs a checkout with a cart id + payment method + idempotency key.
The cart is re-validated and the total recomputed server-side.
A payment intent is created and a pending order is written.
Stripe charges the card and posts a signed webhook with the result.
The webhook (idempotent) flips the order to paid and triggers the receipt email.
decisions: Payment truth is the webhook; the order exists before payment so drops can be reconciled.

stages.title: Create intent + order
stages.desc: Payment intent created; order written pending.

stages.title: Webhook → paid
stages.desc: Signature verified; order flipped to paid.

matrices.title: Order states
rows: Order written, payment not yet confirmed
Charge failed; cart preserved for retry

notes.text: Idempotency end to end: the idempotency key on the request and the webhook handler both make retries safe.
If the webhook is dropped, the order stays 'pending' until a reconcile job polls Stripe — don't treat 'pending' as failed.
